advisory.title: CWD `.env` environment variable injection bypasses host-env policy and allows config takeover
advisory.description:
## Summary

OpenClaw loaded the current working directory `.env` before trusted state-dir configuration, allowing untrusted workspace state to inject host environment values.

## Impact

A repository or workspace containing a malicious `.env` file could override runtime configuration and security-sensitive environment settings when OpenClaw started there.

## Affected Component

`src/infra/dotenv.ts, src/cli/dotenv.ts`

## Fixed Versions

- Affected: `<= 2026.3.24`
- Patched: `>= 2026.3.28`

## Fix

Fixed by commit `6a793248024dca7685f63bcceb64a0096fd1586d` (`Filter untrusted CWD .env entries before OpenClaw startup`).

timeline.notes: This case uses the immediate parent of fix commit 6a793248024dca7685f63bcceb64a0096fd1586d as the last known vulnerable snapshot. Exact introduction commit mapping is still pending.

expectedOutcome.description: Scanner should detect commandinjection when scanning vulnerableHead. This case uses the last known vulnerable pre-fix commit pending exact introduction mapping.

checks.name: vulnerable_head_is_fix_parent
checks.details: Using parent 79fbcfc03b07a260e7ab838e03ffe5863029f19c of fix commit 6a793248024dca7685f63bcceb64a0096fd1586d as the last known vulnerable commit.

checks.name: baseline_precedes_vulnerable_head
checks.details: Using direct parent 501190d2e833215366a72780146926b94c24779c of vulnerableHead 79fbcfc03b07a260e7ab838e03ffe5863029f19c as a temporary baseline until exact introduction mapping is completed.
